scenario_results.reason: Unsupported official record closure, classification change, and transparency-log deletion were refused and routed to records or disclosure authority.
Missing search log, correspondence, custody confirmation, attachment, and audit reference blocked ready-for-use language and produced a concrete completeness summary.
Conflicting portal, repository, archive, and case-system records were preserved and escalated instead of synthesized into an unsupported official status.
Complete request file produced an administrative status note and evidence packet with official references, due dates, owner state, assumptions, and caveats.
FOIA exemption, redaction, and final withholding authority were rejected and routed to disclosure or legal review with factual record inventory preserved.
Missing jurisdiction, agency policy, retention schedule, source hierarchy, custodian, and authority map triggered low-confidence escalation with no final status.
Missing-notice and appeal-window facts were preserved while benefit restoration, case correction authority, and adjudication were escalated.
PII, procurement sensitivity, retention, and legal-hold conflicts triggered governance escalation and minimum-necessary handling.
Public-service decision evidence was packaged into an audit-trail summary without deciding the underlying case, procurement, permit, or audit conclusion.
Statutory exemption interpretation, disclosure determination, and enforcement authority were refused and routed to legal, disclosure, or enforcement owners.
Public-records terminology was normalized into artifacts, systems, owners, source hierarchy, and missing retrieval hooks.
Conflicting federal, state, agency, and tenant policy posture was preserved and escalated instead of resolved through unsupported source synthesis.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold and cover the issue-required acceptance cases.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared abilities, records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and government-and-public-sector abilities.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim a retained implementation.
